required_inputs: request intent and requested publishing operations action
lane scope, adjacent authority map, and approval hierarchy
tenant editorial, legal, rights, accessibility, security, engineering, finance, contractual, and client-approval rules
refusal_triggers: request asks for editorial, legal, rights, accessibility-certification, security, engineering, financial, contractual, policy-override, or final client approval authority
request asks the lane to publish, syndicate, close, or mark complete without required content, metadata, QA, approval, release, delivery, incident, or retention evidence
request asks the lane to bypass confidentiality, embargo, approved-channel, subscriber-data, credential, rights, or tenant-security controls

required_inputs: authoritative W3C, EDItEUR, NISO, IPTC, Crossref, BISG, U.S. Copyright Office, and NIST source set
publication type, content format, metadata workflow, accessibility context, rights context, and system-change context
source recency and decay metadata
refusal_triggers: required content-format, metadata, accessibility, rights, incident, access, change-control, or authority source context is missing
public source, tenant policy, client instruction, partner requirement, SOW, NDA, rights record, or platform rule materially conflicts
source freshness no longer supports a confident publishing operations result

required_inputs: client, publication, title, article, issue, edition, content identifier, slug, canonical URL, DOI, ISBN, version, owner, due date, and system identifiers
editorial handoff, CMS, DAM, metadata, ONIX, JATS, NewsML-G2, EPUB, accessibility, rights, QA, approval, incident, release, delivery, communication, and retention records
completion, incident, change-readiness, and delivery evidence
required_outputs: execution status, incident, or exception summary
refusal_triggers: content package, metadata, asset, rights, accessibility, QA, approval, release, delivery, incident, communication, or retention evidence is incomplete or unverifiable
source records conflict across CMS, DAM, metadata, DOI, feed, partner, ticketing, incident, release, communication, or retention systems
requested state transition or completion is not supported by traceable source evidence

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory
record-location and retrieval hooks
editorial, metadata, DAM, CMS, DOI, feed, subscriber access, incident, release, engineering, QA, accessibility, rights, delivery, client, and retention owner map
required_outputs: blocker summary with missing systems or records
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable or stale
content, metadata, asset, approval, rights, accessibility, access, incident, change, release, delivery, or retention owner is unknown
publishing, metadata, content, access, incident, deployment, partner, communication, audit, or retention source path cannot be retrieved

required_inputs: missing-fact flags
confidence rubric
authority, source-conflict, confidentiality, rights, accessibility, security, incident, approval, contractual, and client-impact indicators
refusal_triggers: critical tenant fact, publication type, content identifier, version, authority record, approval rule, rights rule, accessibility requirement, system record, or source context is unknown
conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, client instruction, SOW, MSA, NDA, rights restriction, partner rule, platform rule, or accessibility requirement remains unresolved
required approval, owner acceptance, rollback, test, or completion evidence is missing for the requested action

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact requirements
required_outputs: handoff package with owner and missing dependencies
refusal_triggers: request is outside publishing operations status, exception, incident, access, change-readiness, metadata, delivery, or completion evidence handling
request requires legal, editorial, rights, accessibility-certification, privacy, security, engineering, finance, account, contractual, policy-override, or final client approval authority
request requires professional judgment or substantive remediation instead of evidence-backed operational handling

required_inputs: accepted work item and target lifecycle state
required content, metadata, QA, approval, release, incident, delivery, and due-date records
tenant state model and completion gates
refusal_triggers: requested state transition lacks required prerequisites
workflow is blocked by missing content, metadata, rights, accessibility, QA, approval, release, access, delivery, or incident evidence
requested transition would bypass editorial, legal, rights, accessibility, security, engineering, client, or contractual review

required_inputs: exception category and affected lifecycle state
content, metadata, asset, accessibility, rights, QA, access, incident, feed, interface, or approval blocker
owner, due date, and escalation threshold
refusal_triggers: exception cannot be mapped to an owner or rule class
source conflict, validation failure, feed failure, access issue, system dependency, confidentiality issue, or approval blocker remains unresolved
request asks to suppress the exception to force publication, release, delivery, or completion

required_inputs: completion target and evidence checklist
publication receipt, package validation, metadata delivery, QA result, approval reference, client delivery record, incident resolution, release note, rollback evidence, and retention references
tenant completion and audit logging rules
refusal_triggers: publication, package, metadata, accessibility, delivery, incident, release, client communication, or retention evidence is missing
approval owner, QA signoff, rights evidence, rollback evidence, access verification, or partner receipt is absent when required
requested completion would misstate actual documented workflow state

required_inputs: client engagement scope and confidentiality records
unpublished content, embargoed asset, subscriber data, platform credential, rights record, analytics, performance-data, approved-recipient, and approved-channel requirements
client commitments, publication dates, delivery obligations, rights constraints, and approval constraints
refusal_triggers: request would disclose or reuse unpublished content, client files, embargoed assets, subscriber data, credentials, rights records, analytics, or performance data without approved scope
requested recipient, channel, purpose, vendor workspace, cross-client folder, portfolio use, or reuse is not approved
client commitment, SOW, MSA, NDA, rights rule, delivery obligation, or tenant policy conflicts with confidentiality, approval, security, or client-rights rules

required_inputs: requested professional, legal, editorial, rights, privacy, accessibility, security, engineering, financial, policy, or contractual action
legal, editorial, accessibility, privacy, security, engineering, rights, delivery, account, finance, contractual, and client approver map
engagement, SOW, MSA, NDA, rights, platform, partner, policy, and approval boundaries
refusal_triggers: request requires legal interpretation, editorial judgment, rights clearance, DMCA disposition, accessibility conformance certification, privacy-law interpretation, security exception approval, engineering remediation, financial approval, final acceptance, or contractual signoff
request changes client commitments, publication obligations, subscriber access obligations, delivery obligations, partner obligations, rights obligations, account terms, or financial exposure
client rights, copyright status, accessibility status, professional judgment, security authority, or final approval authority determine the answer
